title: Palo Alto Networks (PAN-OS) via Syslog via AMA
descriptionMarkdown:
The Palo Alto Networks (PAN-OS) Syslog via AMA connector allows you to ingest PAN-OS firewall logs into Microsoft Sentinel's CommonSecurityLog table using native syslog format with an ingest-time Data Collection Rule (DCR) transformation.

**Why this connector?** Palo Alto Networks has [deprecated CEF logging](https://docs.paloaltonetworks.com/resources/cef) for PAN-OS versions after 10.0. Customers running PAN-OS 10.1 or later should use standard syslog and this connector to continue ingesting logs into CommonSecurityLog.

**How it works:** PAN-OS sends logs in CSV format via syslog to a Linux forwarder running Azure Monitor Agent (AMA). A DCR ingest-time transformation parses the CSV fields and maps them to CommonSecurityLog columns at ingestion. This ensures all existing analytic rules, workbooks, and hunting queries continue to work without modification.

For more information, see the [Palo Alto Networks syslog documentation](https://docs.paloaltonetworks.com/pan-os/11-0/pan-os-admin/monitoring/use-syslog-for-monitoring).

graphQueries.baseQuery:
CommonSecurityLog
| where DeviceVendor == "Palo Alto Networks"
| where DeviceProduct has "PAN-OS"

sampleQueries.query:
CommonSecurityLog
| where DeviceVendor == "Palo Alto Networks"
| where DeviceProduct has "PAN-OS"
| sort by TimeGenerated

CommonSecurityLog
| where DeviceVendor == "Palo Alto Networks"
| where Activity == "TRAFFIC"
| sort by TimeGenerated

CommonSecurityLog
| where DeviceVendor == "Palo Alto Networks"
| where Activity == "THREAT"
| sort by TimeGenerated

value:
CommonSecurityLog
| where DeviceVendor == "Palo Alto Networks"
| where DeviceProduct has "PAN-OS"
| extend sent_by_ama = column_ifexists('CollectorHostName','')
| where isnotempty(sent_by_ama)
| summarize LastLogReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
| project IsConnected = LastLogReceived > ago(3d)

dataTypes.name: CommonSecurityLog (PaloAlto)
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
CommonSecurityLog
| where DeviceVendor == "Palo Alto Networks"
| where DeviceProduct has "PAN-OS"
| extend sent_by_ama = column_ifexists('CollectorHostName','')
| where isnotempty(sent_by_ama)
| summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: read and write permissions are required.

read permissions to shared keys for the workspace are required. [See the documentation to learn more about workspace keys](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/platform/agent-windows#obtain-workspace-id-and-key).

customs.description: To collect data from non-Azure VMs, they must have Azure Arc installed and enabled. [Learn more](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/agents/azure-monitor-agent-install?tabs=ARMAgentPowerShell,PowerShellWindows,PowerShellWindowsArc,CLIWindows,CLIWindowsArc)

instructionSteps.title: 1. Configure PAN-OS Syslog Forwarding
instructionSteps.description:
Configure your Palo Alto firewall to forward logs via syslog (BSD format, CSV payload) to your Linux syslog forwarder.

1. Log in to the Palo Alto Networks firewall web interface.
2. Navigate to **Device > Server Profiles > Syslog** and click **Add**.
3. Enter a name, the IP address of your Linux syslog forwarder, transport (UDP/TCP), port (default 514), and select facility (e.g., `LOG_USER`).
4. Navigate to **Device > Log Settings** and assign the Syslog server profile to the desired log types (Traffic, Threat, System, Config, GlobalProtect).
5. Use the **default (CSV) log format** — do not configure CEF custom log format.
6. Commit the configuration.

For detailed instructions, see [Configure Syslog Monitoring](https://docs.paloaltonetworks.com/pan-os/11-0/pan-os-admin/monitoring/use-syslog-for-monitoring/configure-syslog-monitoring).

instructionSteps.title: 2. Install Azure Monitor Agent (AMA)
instructionSteps.description:
Install the Azure Monitor Agent on your Linux syslog forwarder machine.

1. For Azure VMs, install AMA via the Azure Portal (VM > Extensions > Add Azure Monitor Agent).
2. For non-Azure machines, first install Azure Arc, then install AMA.

[Learn more about AMA installation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/agents/azure-monitor-agent-manage)

instructionSteps.title: 3. Create a Data Collection Rule (DCR) with Ingest-Time Transform
instructionSteps.description:
Create a Data Collection Rule that collects syslog from the forwarder and uses an ingest-time KQL transformation to parse the PAN-OS CSV format into CommonSecurityLog fields.

**DCR Configuration:**
- **Data Source:** Syslog, facility matching your PAN-OS configuration (e.g., `LOG_USER`)
- **Destination:** Your Log Analytics workspace
- **Output Stream:** `Microsoft-CommonSecurityLog`
- **Transform KQL:** Use the transformation below to parse the PAN-OS CSV syslog into CommonSecurityLog columns.

**Ingest-Time Transform KQL:**

```kql
source
| extend Fields = split(SyslogMessage, ",")
| extend
    DeviceVendor = "Palo Alto Networks",
    DeviceProduct = "PAN-OS",
    DeviceVersion = tostring(Fields[0]),
    Activity = case(
        SyslogMessage has "TRAFFIC", "TRAFFIC",
        SyslogMessage has "THREAT", "THREAT",
        SyslogMessage has "SYSTEM", "SYSTEM",
        SyslogMessage has "CONFIG", "CONFIG",
        SyslogMessage has "GLOBALPROTECT", "GLOBALPROTECT",
        "UNKNOWN"
    ),
    DeviceEventClassID = tostring(Fields[4]),
    SourceIP = tostring(Fields[7]),
    DestinationIP = tostring(Fields[8]),
    SourceTranslatedAddress = tostring(Fields[9]),
    DestinationTranslatedAddress = tostring(Fields[10]),
    SourceUserName = tostring(Fields[12]),
    DestinationUserName = tostring(Fields[13]),
    ApplicationProtocol = tostring(Fields[14]),
    SourcePort = toint(Fields[24]),
    DestinationPort = toint(Fields[25]),
    SourceTranslatedPort = toint(Fields[26]),
    DestinationTranslatedPort = toint(Fields[27]),
    Protocol = tostring(Fields[29]),
    DeviceAction = tostring(Fields[30]),
    SentBytes = tolong(Fields[31]),
    ReceivedBytes = tolong(Fields[32]),
    RequestURL = tostring(Fields[39]),
    DeviceCustomString1 = tostring(Fields[11]),
    DeviceCustomString1Label = "RuleName",
    DeviceCustomString2 = tostring(Fields[16]),
    DeviceCustomString2Label = "SourceZone",
    DeviceCustomString3 = tostring(Fields[17]),
    DeviceCustomString3Label = "DestinationZone",
    LogSeverity = tostring(Fields[33]),
    SimplifiedDeviceAction = tostring(Fields[30])
| project-away Fields
```

> **Note:** The field positions above are based on PAN-OS 10.1+ syslog CSV format. Verify against your PAN-OS version's [syslog field descriptions](https://docs.paloaltonetworks.com/pan-os/11-0/pan-os-admin/monitoring/use-syslog-for-monitoring/syslog-field-descriptions).

[Learn more about DCR ingest-time transformations](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/data-collection-transformations)

instructionSteps.title: 4. Validate Data Ingestion
instructionSteps.description:
After configuration, verify that PAN-OS logs are being ingested into CommonSecurityLog:

```kql
CommonSecurityLog
| where DeviceVendor == "Palo Alto Networks"
| where DeviceProduct has "PAN-OS"
| take 10
```

If data appears, all existing analytic rules, workbooks, and hunting queries for Palo Alto PAN-OS will work automatically.
